title: Connections, Channel Ownership, Heartbeats, and Recovery · Runtime Contract: Reconnect Backoff Jitter
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must assign one recovery owner, reject stale generations, drain notifications, and test amqp091-go 1.12 recovery limits; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reconnect backoff jitter increment in a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine. Lea…
objectives: Reconnect with bounded exponential backoff, jitter, cancellation, credential refresh, readiness, and a single recovery owner; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Reconnect Backoff Jitter · Theory Reconnect Backoff Jitter
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 7477 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound heartbeat and recovery delay while rejecting stale channel-generation notifications; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
